behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the specific file (vite.config.ts), the alias target (@cloudflare/style-provider pointing to lib/ CJS build), the base option restriction, assetsDir containing 'playground', the changeset requirement under .changeset/ referencing @cloudflare/workers-playground, and the react/jsx-runtime.js alias preservation. All assert'd paths, strings, and schema keys in tests are discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md explicitly states the exact fix: alias to 'lib/' directory, remove base: '/playground', set assetsDir containing 'playground', and add a changeset. The 'Configuration Requirements' section essentially dictates the solution line by line (e.g., 'must be aliased to a path that routes to its CommonJS build (the lib/ directory inside the package)'). A developer doesn't need to reason about what to change — it's spelled out.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex patterns that allow flexibility: style-provider alias just needs to include 'lib/', assetsDir just needs to include 'playground', base just must not be '/playground'. Alternative implementations (e.g., '@cloudflare/style-provider/lib/index' vs '@cloudflare/style-provider/lib/index.js', assetsDir: 'playground/static' vs 'playground/assets') would all pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While some tests (test_style_provider_cjs_alias, test_no_base_playground_option, test_assets_dir_playground) do text inspection via Node scripts, there are also substantial execution-based tests: test_repo_lint, test_repo_format, test_repo_workers_playground_check, test_repo_workers_playground_build all run actual builds/lints via subprocess. The mix includes real behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for specific config changes that don't exist in the base commit: there's no @cloudflare/style-provider alias pointing to lib/, the base is currently '/playground', there's no assetsDir with 'playground', and no changeset file exists. A stub/no-op solution would fail these tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four explicit requirements from instruction.md are tested: style-provider CJS alias (test_style_provider_cjs_alias), no base '/playground' (test_no_base_playground_option), assetsDir with 'playground' (test_assets_dir_playground), and changeset (test_changeset_for_workers_playground). The build verification tests also confirm the configuration works end-to-end.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with section comments (Gates pass_to_pass static, Gates pass_to_pass repo_tests, Fail-to-pass pr_diff, Config-derived agent_config), each has descriptive docstrings, and naming conventions are consistent and informative.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves config file edits and changeset creation, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, package names, config option names, and commands in instruction.md are all correct and match the actual repository structure.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact file to edit (vite.config.ts), the exact config keys to change (resolve.alias, base, build.assetsDir), and the exact values to use (alias to lib/ directory, remove base: '/playground', assetsDir with 'playground'). This goes far beyond describing symptoms — it prescribes the exact fix in the 'Configuration Requirements' section.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold diff is localized to vite.config.ts (adding an alias, removing base, adding assetsDir) and a new changeset file. It doesn't rewrite the entire file or contradict other configuration.
